activities_projects.title: Area of Triangle with Algebraic Sides
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area using Heron’s formula with algebraic expressions
steps: Take a triangle with sides a = x + 3, b = x + 4, c = x + 5
Calculate semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2
Use Heron’s formula to express area in terms of x
Simplify the expression and record
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula with algebraic side lengths

activities_projects.title: Comparing Areas of Two Triangles with Same Perimeter
activities_projects.objective: Compare areas of triangles with same semi-perimeter but different side lengths
steps: Choose two sets of triangle sides with same s
Calculate area of each using Heron’s formula
Compare and identify which triangle has larger area
Record observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how side lengths affect triangle area

activities_projects.title: Area of Right-Angled Triangle with Heron’s Formula
activities_projects.objective: Verify Heron’s formula matches ½ × base × height
steps: Take triangle with sides 6 cm, 8 cm, 10 cm
Calculate area using ½ × base × height
Also calculate area using Heron’s formula
Compare results
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Confirms Heron’s formula works for right-angled triangles

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangle with Decimal Sides
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of triangle with decimal side lengths
steps: Take triangle with sides a = 5.7 cm, b = 6.3 cm, c = 4.8 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter s = (a + b + c)/2
Use Heron’s formula to find area
Record result in notebook
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula with decimal side lengths

activities_projects.title: Triangle Area using Perimeter Constraint
activities_projects.objective: Find maximum area triangle for given perimeter using Heron’s formula
steps: Take perimeter P = 12 cm
Choose multiple sets of triangle sides with sum 12 cm
Calculate area for each set using Heron’s formula
Identify which combination gives maximum area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Explores relationship between perimeter and maximum area

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangle with Mixed Units
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area using Heron’s formula with sides in different units
steps: Take sides a = 5 m, b = 300 cm, c = 0.04 km
Convert all sides into same unit
Calculate semi-perimeter s
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula with unit conversion

activities_projects.title: Triangle Area using Graph Paper
activities_projects.objective: Draw triangle on graph paper and calculate area using Heron’s formula
steps: Draw triangle with sides measured using graph scale
Measure sides using ruler
Calculate semi-perimeter and area using Heron’s formula
Compare with approximate visual area on graph
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula practically using drawing

activities_projects.title: Exploring Heron’s Formula for Isosceles Triangle
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area for various isosceles triangles and compare
steps: Take isosceles triangles with sides a = b = 5 cm, c = 6 cm; a = b = 7 cm, c = 4 cm
Calculate semi-perimeter and area for each using Heron’s formula
Compare areas and note observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands effect of unequal base on area

activities_projects.title: Area of Triangle Formed by Intersecting Lines
activities_projects.objective: Calculate area of triangle formed by intersection of given lines using distances as sides
steps: Draw three lines intersecting to form triangle
Measure side lengths
Calculate semi-perimeter and area using Heron’s formula
Record result
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies Heron’s formula in coordinate or drawing context

activities_projects.title: Real-Life Triangle Area Estimation
activities_projects.objective: Estimate and calculate area of triangular plots or objects using Heron’s formula
steps: Measure three sides of a triangular object or plot
Calculate semi-perimeter
Use Heron’s formula to calculate area
Compare with actual area if possible
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects Heron’s formula to real-life applications
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate measurement and area calculation (10 marks)
